elements: motion vectors on the image plane

interactions.label: Translation Distance
interactions.teaches: Adjusts the camera's lateral translation distance, affecting the motion perceived on the image plane.

interactions.label: Show Depth Layers
interactions.teaches: Reveals the different 3D layers to show how depth impacts apparent motion on the image plane.

demo_steps.title: No Lateral Movement
demo_steps.narration: Initially, the camera is not moving. All points on the image plane remain static regardless of their depth. This sets the stage to understand lateral motion effects.

demo_steps.title: Lateral Translation Effects
demo_steps.narration: As the camera begins to translate laterally, observe how objects appear to move in the opposite direction on the image plane. Turn on the depth layers to see the differing motions.

demo_steps.title: Depth Influences Motion
demo_steps.narration: With increased lateral translation, the parallax effect is more pronounced. Objects closer to the camera move significantly compared to those farther away, illustrating how depth influences motion perception.

plan.camera_suggestion: Positioned to view from the side, capturing both the motion field on the image plane and the depth variation, with a moderate zoom to highlight changes vividly.
plan.notes: Utilize transparent layers to visually differentiate depth, and ensure vectors in the motion field accurately reflect direction and magnitude changes with translation.

interactions.label: Lateral Translation
interactions.teaches: Visualizes the effect of lateral camera motion on the image plane.

demo_steps.title: Start with No Translation
demo_steps.narration: Here, the camera is stationary. Notice that the elements in the image plane are not moving. This is the baseline scenario before any lateral motion.

demo_steps.title: Introduce Lateral Motion
demo_steps.narration: Now, let's introduce lateral camera motion. As the camera moves sideways, objects in the image plane appear to move in the opposite direction.

demo_steps.title: Enhanced Parallax Effect
demo_steps.narration: As the lateral motion increases, nearby objects appear to move faster than distant ones, demonstrating the parallax effect. This variance in motion can help infer depth.

interactionPlan.camera_suggestion: Front view centered on image plane, encompassing entire motion field with a moderate zoom level.
interactionPlan.notes: Ensure smooth interpolation for slider changes to vividly show the parallax effect.
